D11 ComputationalL3 ApplyReference

YCoCg Decorrelation — Reversible RGB Transform

Y = (R + 2G + B)/4, Co = (R − B)/2, Cg = G − (R + B)/2; integer-reversible, decorrelates better than YCbCr — used in HEVC/AV1 internally

RGB channels are highly correlated (R and G have correlation ~0.7 on natural images), so coding them independently wastes bits. YCoCg-R is a reversible lift that reduces inter-channel correlation, packs energy into Y, and uses smaller bit ranges for Co/Cg in flat regions. Codecs run their entropy coder on YCoCg, not RGB.

Toggle channel to visualise.
